task_completion.description: Verify the survey is created with the correct title and welcome/end messages.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the survey title 'Customer Experience Journey Survey Q4 2024' in the LimeSurvey interface. Also, check for the welcome message containing 'Customer Experience study' and the end message containing 'feedback helps us improve' in the text editor or survey settings.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Purchase Details' group contains the correct Date/Time and Numerical Input questions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a question group named 'Purchase Details' in the survey structure. It should contain a Date/Time question ('When did your most recent purchase take place?') and a Numerical Input question ('Approximate total amount spent (USD)'). The numerical question settings should show a minimum value of 1 and a maximum value of 10000.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Experience Evaluation' group contains the 5 Point Choice Array question with correct sub-questions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a question group named 'Experience Evaluation'. It must contain a 5 Point Choice Array question ('Please rate your satisfaction...') with sub-questions visible for Store Environment, Staff Helpfulness, Product Availability, Checkout Experience, and Value for Money.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Experience Evaluation' group contains the Ranking question with correct options.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Ranking question ('Please rank the following factors...') within the 'Experience Evaluation' group. The answer options should include Price, Product Quality, Convenience, Customer Service, and Product Variety.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Feedback' group contains the Multiple Choice and Long Free Text questions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a question group named 'Feedback'. It must contain a Multiple Choice question ('How did you first learn about our store?') with options like Social Media, Word of Mouth, etc., and a Long Free Text question ('Please share any additional comments...').

task_completion.description: Verify that the survey has been activated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a status indicator showing the survey is 'Active' (often a green checkmark or badge) or the presence of a 'Stop this survey' button in the top toolbar, indicating the activation process was completed.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the LimeSurvey web interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show a web browser navigating the LimeSurvey admin dashboard, not terminal commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: Verify the questions and groups were actually created within LimeSurvey.
integrity.visual_evidence: The survey elements should be visible in the LimeSurvey structure sidebar or question list, rather than just typed into a text editor or a single generic text field.

integrity.description: Verify the UI has not been manipulated using browser developer tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: The LimeSurvey interface should look standard without misaligned elements, injected text overlays, or open developer console windows.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected Survey Title: 'Customer Experience Journey Survey Q4 2024'. Expected Welcome Snippet: 'Customer Experience study'. Expected End Snippet: 'feedback helps us improve'. The survey must contain exactly 6 questions across 3 groups. The numerical question must have a minimum value of 1 and a maximum value of 10000. Ensure the Long Free Text question is visually verified in the 'Feedback' group, as it is required by the task.
